title: festuca ophioliticola subsp. armoricana
intro: Festuca ophioliticola subsp. armoricana, also known as Armorican fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is native to Armorica, a historical region in northwestern France, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin irritations, thanks to its flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins content. Festuca ophioliticola subsp. armoricana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
